repo_prior_art.cite: panel_sdid_mc_id_v1: spectral equality/frontier proposals plus API panel spectral-threshold entries, but no low-rank MC margin theorem

published_prior_art.cite: SingerCucuringu2010 and Chatterjee2020: deterministic low-rank completion uniqueness/solvability without causal ATT or estimator-duality layer

repo_prior_art.cite: panel_sdid_mc_id_v1: sharp estimator-level SDiD/ASCM/MC equality frontier; current margin is more observable/staggered but same internal angle family

published_prior_art.cite: AtheyBayatiDoudchenkoImbensKhosravi2021, ArkhangelskyAtheyHirshbergImbensWager2021, SingerCucuringu2010, Chatterjee2020: none gives a finite staggered-mask iff plus MC--SDiD--ASCM first-order-law equality

repo_prior_art.cite: panel_sdid_mc_id_v1: open-set separation outside SDiD/ASCM/MC equality frontier; current below-threshold staggered mask is sharper but adjacent

published_prior_art.cite: none: deterministic completion papers give failure/uniqueness conditions but not open-class causal ATT nonidentification with estimator-law separation

repo_prior_art.cite: panel_sdid_mc_id_v1: penalty-stable equality frontier for ASCM/SDiD/MC; current tuning-neutral margin interval is a refined variant

published_prior_art.cite: Heiniger2024 and BaiNg2021: model selection/factor imputation, not common tuning-neutral MC--SDiD--ASCM first-order law

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The below-threshold open-class nonidentification claim has no concrete non-corner staggered witness in §9; the only concrete witness is positive-margin, so flagship tier is capped at field until a below-threshold instance is exhibited.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The survey misses Choi and Yuan 2024 JASA, 'Matrix Completion When Missing Is Not at Random and Its Applications in Causal Panel Data Models,' which directly treats structured/staggered causal-panel missingness via nuclear-norm subproblems.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 3
novelty_flags.one_line: The tuning-path claim cites Heiniger2024 and BaiNg2021 only by bibkey in §8, with no section/equation/page anchor tying them to the specific tuning-neutrality gap.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The open-class separation claim cites SingerCucuringu2010, Chatterjee2020, and BenMichaelFellerRothstein2022 without location-level anchors for the specific failure/nonidentification gap.

soundness_flags.label: Random-mask reduction
soundness_flags.one_line: With the proposal's own scalings m_r(D)≈pΩNT and b(D)≈pΩ, lambda_r scales like sqrt(r)/(pΩ^{3/2}sqrt(NT)); at the Candès--Tao sampling rate this does not reduce to the classical threshold up to logs.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1 / Conjecture 3
soundness_flags.one_line: The SDiD and ridge ASCM first-order laws are referenced as published balancing objectives but the proposal never pins the exact population objective/normalization/penalty convention needed for equality of a_sdid and a_ascm.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add a concrete below-threshold staggered rank-r witness with named N,T,r, adoption dates, observed mask, two completions, and a displayed ATT separation direction.

Repair the random-mask corollary by recalculating b(D), m_r(D), ell(D), lambda_r(D), and gamma_r(D) so the threshold algebra genuinely matches Candès--Tao/Keshavan rates, or downgrade the corollary to a qualitative analogy.
Insert explicit population SDiD and ridge-ASCM objective formulas, including normalizations and penalty limits, before stating first-order law equality.
Add Choi--Yuan 2024 to related work and state precisely why their staggered/MNAR nuclear-norm inference does not subsume the proposed estimator-duality threshold.

recommended_next_step: Revise locally: add the below-threshold witness, fix the random-mask algebra, and pin the SDiD/ASCM objective definitions before resubmitting for flagship review.
